troversy by forcing the adoption Of
fair free speech ordinance
through the medium Of the initia.
tive charter amendment adopted

40 Il 10 becoming fully understood.


The Industrial Workers of the
World, whose members have gone
to Jail as protest against the pres
ent ordinance, are taking no hand
in the matter and are leaving It en-
tirely to citizens and the different
labor organizations to push along.
The l. w. w. feel that by filling
the jails and courts they may be
able to secure a reconsideration OF
the street speaking ordinance. The
members of the labor unions are
ess militant and feel that the peo-
pie, when they adopted the charter
amendment last May providing for
the initiative and referendum.
placed q weapon In the hands Of
the people by which they can COM
pei the officials at the city hall to
exercise judgment in handling such
matters.


At the meeting of the Central
Labor council last night the matter
of adjusting the street speaking
controversy by an appeal to the
initiative was discussed and met
